name: Jack Lesberg
abstract: Jack Lesberg (February 14, 1920 – September 17, 2005) was an American jazz double-bassist. Lesberg performed with many famous jazz musicians, including Louis Armstrong, Earl Hines, Jack Teagarden, Sarah Vaughan and Benny Goodman, with whom he went on several international tours.

interviews.title: Jack Lesberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

works.t: Love is Just Around the Corner

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Salute to Eddie Condon
Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Teddy Wilson Trio
Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Chicago Jazz Sextet
JVC Jazz Festival: Jazz and The American Song

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: It says, Autum of '57 you toured Europe with 'All Stars' led by Jack Teagarden and Earl Hines. ... It was Jack Teagarden, Earl Hines were heading the group, was Peanuts Hucko; Max Kaminsky; Jack Lesberg and me.

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph Sutton
about_quotes.evidence: And in the band he had Max Kaminsky, Jack Lesberg, Peanuts Hucko, Eddie Shaughnessy on drums and myself on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph Sutton interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: I worked there with Jack Lesberg. He and I -- he was bass drummer and I was the house drummer... We had Peanuts Hucko for a leader

about_quotes.speaker: Stanley Kay
about_quotes.evidence: And when Jack Lesberg came in the band I grew a hundred times, playing with a good bass player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Stanley Kay interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: James Chirillo
about_quotes.evidence: Jack Lesberg was playing bass — first time I ever got a chance to play with Jack.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Chirillo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: We had Peanuts, Trummy Young, Billy Butterfield, Jack Lesberg and Gus Johnson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Napoleon interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, New York, January 16, 1999
